mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% indicates a tight labor market, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 30% of triggered instances despite falling UNRATE, or t-test p > 0.1 for mean returns in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly expansion in GDP (GDPC1) reflects strong aggregate demand, which supports corporate profits and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in < 50% of GDP expansion periods, or GDP growth decouples from equity returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) combined with stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals robust supply-side growth and favorable monetary conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO/CPIAUCSL interactions, or Rank IC < 0.05 for combined signals in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts (HOUST) above 1,350 thousand units indicates strong demand for residential construction, boosting related sectors and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 35% of triggered HOUST spikes, or HOUST growth decouples from equity returns in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) and elevated inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a monetary tightening cycle, which raises borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS/CPIAUCSL co-movements, or Rank IC > 0.05 for combined signals in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects strong labor market conditions, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in < 40% of PAYEMS growth periods, or t-test p > 0.05 for mean returns in strong labor markets.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates robust supply-side activity, which supports corporate profits and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in < 30% of INDPRO growth periods, or INDPRO growth decouples from equity returns in 3-year rolling windows.
